steps.title: Identify what changes between the two sets
steps.content: Ari examined 86 plants with masses in the range [2.8 kg, 6.2 kg]. Chihiro examined the same 86 plants plus one additional plant weighing 11.7 kg.

steps.title: Determine how the mean changes
steps.content:
Since all of Ari's plants are in the range [2.8, 6.2] kg, the mean of Ari's set is at most 6.2 kg.

The new plant weighs 11.7 kg, which is greater than 6.2 kg and therefore greater than any possible mean of Ari's original set.

When you add a value above the current mean to a data set, the mean increases.

Therefore, Chihiro's mean must be greater than Ari's mean. That matches Choice D — The mean of the masses, in kg, of the plants that Ari examined is less than the mean of the masses, in kg, of the plants that Chihiro examined..

steps.title: Check the range distractor
steps.content: Ari's range is $6.2-2.8=3.4$ kg. Chihiro adds a plant weighing $11.7$ kg, so Chihiro's range is $11.7-2.8=8.9$ kg. Ari's range is not greater than Chihiro's range, so Choice B is false.
